summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Maromba Hacker visual novel game. Core gameplay mechanics fully functional with excellent mobile-first design and cyberpunk theming. Minor UI feedback issue with error messages.

ui_bugs.issue: Error messages not visible when wrong equipment selected or no equipment selected for required steps

passed_tests: Initial screen loads with logo, mascot, and 'MILÍCIA DIGITAL' title
START button navigation to mission selection screen
All 5 missions available with correct colored zones (3 green, 1 yellow, 1 red)
Mission selection opens game screen with story and equipment grid
Equipment grid displays all 7 hacker tools with proper icons
Equipment selection works with visual feedback (green glow)
Step 1 validation - requires and accepts Arduino
Step 2 validation - requires and accepts Pen Drive
Mission completion screen with rewards and mascot quote
Navigation buttons work (NEXT and OTHER MISSIONS)
All missions accessible (Presente Grego, Internet Popular, TV Hackeada, Cafézinho Especial, Pedófilo Exposto)
Zone color coding correct (Verde, Amarela, Vermelha)
Visual effects present (scanlines, map background, green glow, dark overlay)
Fixed footer with logo and branding
Equipment hints system working ('DICA: Selecione o...')
Step progression flow (Step 1 → Step 2 → Step 3 → Completion)
Cyberpunk theming with proper fonts and colors
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix error message visibility in step validation. Messages should appear when users select wrong equipment or attempt to complete steps without required equipment. The validation logic exists but messages are not visible to users.
